Home / SaaS Financial Forecaster

SaaS Financial Forecaster

The complete SaaS financial health dashboard: net-growth projections, runway, breakeven, burn metrics, LTV, sensitivity analysis, and what-if scenarios.

🎭 Scenario Presets

How to Use This Tool

1
Enter your current Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR).
2
Enter your average monthly growth rate (gross, before churn).
3
Enter your monthly churn rate to see net growth — the real number.
4
Enter your total monthly expenses (hosting, tools, salary, etc.).
5
Enter your cash on hand to calculate runway and financial buffer.
6
Enter your ARPU (Average Revenue Per User) for subscriber counts.
7
Optionally enter a custom growth rate to test your own scenario.
8
Optionally enter your CAC (Customer Acquisition Cost) for payback and LTV:CAC.
9
Select your projection period (6, 12, or 24 months).
10
Review the Revenue Snapshot for your net growth and ending MRR.
11
Scan the Monthly MRR Breakdown to see month-by-month progress.
12
Check Runway & Breakeven to know how long your cash will last.
13
Study the Burn & Efficiency Metrics — Rule of 40, Burn Multiple, LTV, CAC ratios.
14
Explore What-If scenarios to see the impact of better churn or lower costs.
15
Read the Action Plan for prioritized next steps based on your data.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is a good monthly growth rate for a SaaS?

For early-stage SaaS (under $10K MRR), 5-10% monthly net growth is good. At $10K-$100K MRR, 3-5% is healthy. Above $100K MRR, 2-3% is normal. Net growth = gross growth − churn — both matter equally. This calculator shows you your net growth and projects it forward with five growth scenarios.

What's the difference between gross growth and net growth?

Gross growth = new customer revenue + expansion revenue (upgrades). Net growth = gross growth − churn (cancellations + downgrades). If your gross growth is 8% but churn is 5%, your net is only 3% — growing but losing nearly half to churn. Net growth is what builds long-term revenue.

How do I calculate my monthly expenses?

Include everything that recurs monthly: hosting, SaaS tools, contractor/VA costs, software subscriptions, marketing budget, and your own salary/withdrawal. Don't include one-time purchases. If your expenses fluctuate, use the 3-month average.

What's a healthy runway for a solopreneur?

12+ months of runway at current burn rate is comfortable. 6-12 months is manageable but requires attention. Under 6 months is critical — you need to cut costs, raise prices, or launch new revenue streams immediately. The calculator shows both 'zero-revenue' and 'current pace' runway so you can plan for the worst case while tracking the realistic one.

What is Burn Multiple and why does it matter?

Burn Multiple = net burn ÷ net new MRR. It measures how much you spend to generate each dollar of new recurring revenue. <1.0× is excellent (efficient growth), 1.0-2.0× is acceptable, >2.0× means over-spending relative to growth. Profitable businesses have no burn multiple. Track this monthly — a rising burn multiple is an early warning sign.

How accurate are these revenue projections?

The projection is mathematically precise given your inputs, but real-world growth is lumpy — some months up 12%, some flat. Use the conservative and aggressive scenarios to see the range of possible outcomes. For budgeting, always plan with the conservative end. Revisit this calculator monthly to update with your actual numbers.

What's a good LTV:CAC ratio?

A 3:1 LTV:CAC ratio is the SaaS industry benchmark — your customer lifetime value should be at least 3× your cost to acquire them. 5:1 or higher is excellent (you can afford to spend more on acquisition). Below 1:1 means losing money on every customer. Track CAC Payback alongside: under 12 months to recover your acquisition cost is healthy.

Results

Example

📊 Revenue Snapshot

• Starting MRR: $5,000/mo • Ending MRR: $8,979/mo (after 12 months) • ARR Equivalent: $107,751/yr • Total Revenue: $83,565 over 12 months • Gross Monthly Growth: +8.0% (new + expansion) • Monthly Churn: −3.0% (lost revenue) • Net Monthly Growth: +5.0% (effective) 🟡 Healthy • Growth Multiple: 1.8× (12-month MRR expansion) 📈 MRR Milestones • Q1 (Month 3): $5,788/mo • Q2 (Month 6): $6,700/mo • Q3 (Month 9): $7,757/mo • Q4 (Month 12): $8,979/mo ← target 🎯 Key Milestones • $10K MRR: 15 months [11–24] • $25K MRR: 33 months [24–55] • $50K MRR: 48 months [35–78] • $100K MRR: >5 years Range shows ±2% net growth variation. 📅 Monthly MRR Breakdown Month 1: $5,250/mo (+$250) Month 2: $5,513/mo (+$263) Month 3: $5,788/mo (+$276) ← Q1 Month 4: $6,078/mo (+$289) Month 5: $6,381/mo (+$304) Month 6: $6,700/mo (+$319) ← Q2 Month 7: $7,036/mo (+$335) Month 8: $7,387/mo (+$352) Month 9: $7,757/mo (+$369) ← Q3 Month 10: $8,144/mo (+$388) Month 11: $8,552/mo (+$407) Month 12: $8,979/mo (+$428) ← Q4 🔄 Growth Scenarios (12-Month Outlook) • 🐢 Conservative +3.0%/mo → $7,129/mo | $10K in 24 mo • 📈 Current Pace +5.0%/mo → $8,979/mo | $10K in 15 mo • 🚀 Aggressive +8.0%/mo → $12,591/mo | $10K in 10 mo • 🔥 Hyper-Growth +10.0%/mo → $15,692/mo | $10K in 8 mo • 🎯 Custom: enter a growth rate above to see your target 🔄 What-If Analysis A) Cut churn 3.0% → 2.0%: Net growth: +5.0% → +6.0% | End MRR: $8,979 → $10,061 (+$1,082) B) Boost gross growth +20%: Gross: +8.0% → +9.6% | End MRR: $8,979 → $10,766 (+$1,787) C) Cut expenses 20%: Expenses: $3,000/mo → $2,400/mo | Monthly savings: +$600/mo Profit increases: $2,000/mo → $2,600/mo 💰 Runway & Breakeven • Cash on Hand: $60,000 • Monthly Expenses: $3,000/mo • Monthly Net Revenue: $5,000/mo (MRR) • Monthly Profit: +$2,000 🟢 Revenue covers expenses. • Runway (zero-revenue): 20.0 months • Runway (current pace): ∞ (profitable) • Breakeven: ✅ Already breakeven • Annualized Profit: +$24,000/yr 🩺 Burn & Efficiency Metrics • Gross Burn: $3,000/mo (total expenses) • Net Burn: +$2,000/mo (profit) 🟢 • Burn Multiple: N/A (profitable) • Rule of 40: 45.0% 🟢 = net growth 5.0% + profit margin 40.0% | ≥40% 🟢 | 20-40% 🟡 | <20% 🔴 • MRR / Expense Ratio: 1.67× 🟡 ≥2.0 🟢 | 1.0-2.0 🟡 | <1.0 🔴 • LTV (Customer Lifetime): $833 (33× ARPU) 🟡 = ARPU ÷ monthly churn | ≥36× 🟢 | 12-36× 🟡 | <12× 🔴 • CAC (Customer Acq. Cost): $200 • CAC Payback Period: 8.2 months 🟢 = CAC ÷ (ARPU × (1 − churn rate)) | <12mo 🟢 | 12-24mo 🟡 | >24mo 🔴 • LTV:CAC Ratio: 4.2× 🟢 = LTV ÷ CAC | ≥3× 🟢 | 1-3× 🟡 | <1× 🔴 • Monthly ARPU: $25 • Subscribers: 200 (currentMRR ÷ ARPU) 🎯 Action Plan • Stage: Early Traction ($1K–$10K MRR) • Burn: ✅ Profitable — reinvest 30% of profit into growth. • Growth: 🚀 Strong (+5.0% net) — compound is working for you. • Risk: 🟢 Low — profitable + growing. Focus on moat and scale. 🔥 Top Priorities: 1. Cut churn from 3.0% → 2.0% → $10K MRR sooner. 2. With $2,000/mo profit, reinvest in ads or part-time help. ⚖️ Break-Even Revenue • 🟢 Break-even MRR: $3,000/mo (covers monthly expenses) • Current MRR $5,000 is above break-even by $2,000/mo.
